Taking care of your sexual health is part of taking care of your whole body.
You can protect yourself by:
Getting regular STI tests
— Many infections don’t show symptoms, so testing and staying up to date on vaccines helps catch them early.
Knowing your resources
— From birth control counseling to prenatal vitamins, support is available to you!
Talking to a trusted adult or healthcare professional
— They can answer your questions and guide you to the right care.
For a quick look at all the sexual health services we offer,
click here
.
What is an STI and how can I avoid getting one?
Most people with an STI don’t feel sick or see signs. That’s why testing is so important. Testing finds infections early, so you can get treated and avoid spreading them.
Learn more here
.
You can get tested at Mono County Public Health.
No stress, no guess—get an STI test!
Vaccines
are another great way to protect yourself. There are vaccines for HPV, hepatitis A, hepatitis B, and mpox. Scientists are also working on vaccines for HIV and herpes. Vaccines are one of the best ways to prevent serious illness—and many are free or low-cost.
Learn more here
.
Birth Control Counseling
Learning about birth control can feel overwhelming, but you don’t have to figure it out alone. Whether you’re new or already know a lot, we’re here to answer questions and share clear info about your options—so you can choose what’s right for you.
What else is “sexual health”?
Sexual health isn’t only about preventing STIs or pregnancy. It’s also about feeling safe, respected, and cared for in your relationships. A healthy relationship means listening to each other, making choices together, and treating each other with kindness.
Learn more about healthy relationships here
.
If you’re worried about your sexual health or the safety of your relationship, please contact Mono County Public Health at
760-924-1830
, or talk with a trusted adult or healthcare professional right away. You can also reach out to the resources below for support:
Wild Iris
— Free, confidential support for anyone facing domestic violence, sexual assault, or child abuse.
wild-iris.org
24/7 Call/Text Crisis Line:
1-877-873-7384
Call to make an appointment at your preferred Mono County location:
760-924-1830
